articles.title: 30 Million Want Mbappe Out of Real Madrid But Here Is Why
articles.summary: A petition titled 'Mbappe Out' has exploded past 30 million signatures, calling for Real Madrid to sell Kylian Mbappe at the end of the season. The backlash stems from a combination of a trophyless two seasons, a training ground bust-up, and photos of Mbappe on a yacht in Sardinia while his teammates played. However, experts are questioning whether the numbers are genuine, with French newspaper Le Parisien reporting that bots may be inflating the count.
articles.content:
A petition called 'Mbappe Out' has gone completely viral, racking up over 30 million signatures and calling on Real Madrid to sell Kylian Mbappe this summer. The petition originally aimed for just 200,000 responses. It blew past that almost instantly. If the signatures are all real people, Sky Sports chief correspondent Kaveh Solhekol notes it would make it a world record-breaking petition.

But here is the catch. The petition was built on Replit, a cloud-based app platform, and it requires no email address or personal details to sign. Just one click. French newspaper Le Parisien has reported that security experts believe bots could be artificially inflating the numbers through automated tools. So while the anger at Mbappe is real, 30 million genuine Real Madrid fans signing a petition in a matter of days is almost certainly not the full picture.

Still, the frustration behind the petition is very real. Kylian Mbappe joined Real Madrid on a free transfer from Paris Saint-Germain in 2024, and despite scoring 85 goals in around 100 appearances, Real Madrid have not won a single major trophy since he arrived. To make things worse, PSG won the Champions League for the first time ever in the season after Mbappe left, and PSG are back in the final again this month. Real Madrid, meanwhile, are out of the Champions League, out of the Copa del Rey, and trail Barcelona by 11 points in La Liga with only four rounds to go.

The final straw for many fans was seeing photos of Mbappe on a yacht in Sardinia with actress Ester Exposito while he was supposed to be recovering from a hamstring injury. His teammates Vinicius Jr and Jude Bellingham were playing against Espanyol at the same time. The optics were terrible. Reports from The Athletic also revealed that Mbappe had a heated dispute with a coaching staff member during a training exercise, reportedly speaking in insulting terms toward someone acting as an assistant referee who called him offside. That did not help.

There are also genuine tactical questions about whether Real Madrid simply work better without Mbappe in the starting lineup. As Bleacher Report noted, Real Madrid actually played some of their best football this season without him. The issue of whether Mbappe and Vinicius Jr can coexist effectively has been a running debate since day one, with both players naturally occupying similar spaces on the pitch.

As for what happens next, almost nothing, at least in the short term. Mbappe is under contract at Real Madrid until 2029. His estimated transfer fee would be around £200 million, and under the new Squad Cost Ratio rules in football, finding a buyer would be extremely difficult. The petition is loud. The exit door is locked.
keyPoints: A petition titled 'Mbappe Out' has surpassed 30 million signatures calling for Real Madrid to sell Kylian Mbappe
The petition requires no login or verification, and Le Parisien reports bots may be inflating the numbers
Real Madrid have won no major trophies in the two seasons since Mbappe joined from PSG in 2024
Fan anger escalated after photos emerged of Mbappe on a yacht in Sardinia with actress Ester Exposito while injured
Mbappe is contracted to Real Madrid until 2029, making any exit extremely unlikely in the near future

articles.title: Stanford Study: 35% of New Websites Are Now AI-Generated
articles.summary: A new study from Stanford University, Imperial College London, and the Internet Archive has put a precise number on the Dead Internet Theory: 35.3% of all newly published websites as of mid-2025 were AI-generated or AI-assisted, up from zero before ChatGPT launched in November 2022. Nearly half of that figure, 17.6% of all new sites, were entirely generated by AI with no human input. The study's most alarming finding may not be what you expect: the real danger is not misinformation but model collapse, where future AI systems train on AI-generated data and begin to degrade.
articles.content:
You have probably heard the phrase "Dead Internet Theory" floating around online. The idea is this: the internet we once knew, full of real people writing real things, has been slowly replaced by bots and automated content until what is left is mostly machines talking to machines. It started as a fringe theory, but a major new study just gave it a very uncomfortable number.

Researchers from Stanford University, Imperial College London, and the Internet Archive collaborated on a paper titled "The Impact of AI-Generated Text on the Internet." They used the Internet Archive's Wayback Machine to analyse web pages published between 2022 and 2025, running them through an AI text detector called Pangram v3. Their finding: as of May 2025, 35.3% of all newly published websites were AI-generated or AI-assisted. That includes 17.6% that were entirely produced by AI with zero human involvement. Before ChatGPT launched in November 2022, that figure was essentially zero.

Researcher Jonáš Doležal of Imperial College London told 404 Media: "I find the sheer speed of the AI takeover of the web quite staggering. After decades of humans shaping it, a significant portion of the internet has become defined by AI in just three years." The findings also line up with Cloudflare's separate report that nearly a third of all internet traffic now comes from bots, and with data from security firm Imperva showing that automated traffic surpassed human traffic for the first time in 2024.

Here is the twist though: the study actually debunked several of the most popular fears about AI content. The most widely held belief, shared by 83% of survey respondents, was that AI is flattening the internet into a stylistic monoculture where everything sounds the same. The data found no statistically significant evidence that this is happening. The researchers also found no meaningful link between AI prevalence and factual inaccuracy, meaning AI content is not measurably more full of lies than human content.

What the study did confirm is subtler but arguably more serious. AI content is making the internet less semantically diverse, meaning fewer original ideas and a narrower range of perspectives. It is also making content more artificially positive and upbeat in tone. And the deepest long-term concern is model collapse: at 35% AI prevalence, future AI systems that train on today's web will inevitably be training on vast amounts of AI-generated data, producing models that are measurably less capable and diverse. A survey conducted alongside the study found that people who use AI infrequently were 12% more likely to believe in its harms than frequent users, suggesting that fear of AI content is often disconnected from actual experience with it.

The Dead Internet is not here yet. But 35% of what is new on the web right now is, in some sense, not human. That number will only go up, and the implications for the next generation of AI models, trained on this same web, are a genuine and documented risk.
keyPoints: 35.3% of all newly published websites as of mid-2025 are AI-generated or AI-assisted
17.6% of new websites are entirely AI-generated with no human involvement whatsoever
The figure was essentially zero before ChatGPT launched in November 2022
The study debunked the popular fear that AI is making the internet less factually accurate
The real confirmed danger is model collapse: future AI systems will train heavily on AI-generated data and degrade over time

articles.title: Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace Make History in Europe
articles.summary: Arsenal, Aston Villa, and Crystal Palace have all reached European finals in the same season, making England the first nation ever to achieve this feat. Arsenal will face Paris Saint-Germain in the Champions League final, Aston Villa take on Freiburg in the Europa League final, and Crystal Palace meet Rayo Vallecano in the Conference League final. It is a landmark moment for English football that has never been matched by any country in the history of the three competitions running simultaneously.
articles.content:
English football just wrote a page in the history books. <cite index="12-2">Arsenal, Aston Villa, and Crystal Palace have all qualified for European finals in the same season, making England the first nation to achieve this feat.</cite> It is a clean sweep across all three of UEFA's major club competitions, and it happened in the space of just a few days.

<cite index="12-3">Arsenal sealed their place in the Champions League final with a 2-1 aggregate victory against Atletico Madrid on Tuesday.</cite> <cite index="11-4">They will face Paris Saint-Germain, the defending champions, at the Puskás Aréna in Budapest, Hungary, on 30 May 2026.</cite> <cite index="18-2">The Gunners, who were runners-up in 2006, are seeking to become the 25th club to lift the trophy.</cite>

<cite index="14-9">Unai Emery's Aston Villa side overturned a 1-0 first-leg defeat to thrash Nottingham Forest 4-1 on aggregate on Thursday, setting up a Europa League final meeting with Bundesliga side Freiburg in Istanbul on 20 May.</cite> <cite index="14-10,14-11">Simultaneously, Crystal Palace followed up a 3-1 victory against Shakhtar Donetsk the week before with a 2-1 win at Selhurst Park in the second leg. They will face Rayo Vallecano in the Conference League final on 27 May.</cite> The Conference League, by the way, is UEFA's third-tier club competition, introduced in 2021 to give more clubs a route into European football.

<cite index="13-8,13-9,13-10">This achievement marks only the second time in history that a single national association has been represented in the final of all three major European tournaments in the same year. The first instance was in 2022/23, when Italy sent Inter Milan, AS Roma, and Fiorentina to the three respective finals. However, the Italian sides were defeated in all three matches.</cite> England now has a chance to go one better and actually win the lot.

<cite index="16-5">This season was already historic before the finals were set, as it was the first Champions League campaign to feature six clubs from one nation, with England earning an extra spot through UEFA coefficient ranking.</cite> The Premier League's dominance this year has been total. Three finals, three English clubs, three chances to bring silverware home.
keyPoints: Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have all reached European finals in 2025/26, a first for any nation
Arsenal face Paris Saint-Germain in the Champions League final in Budapest on 30 May
Aston Villa face Freiburg in the Europa League final in Istanbul on 20 May
Crystal Palace face Rayo Vallecano in the Conference League final on 27 May
Italy reached all three finals in 2022/23 but lost every single one
articles.wikiSearchQuery: 2026 UEFA Champions League final Arsenal PSG

articles.title: Ariana Grande Was Too Shy to Make Petal, Then Said 'F-ck It'
articles.summary: Ariana Grande has announced her new album Petal, due July 31, revealing in an Instagram video that she was initially too shy to release it. Grande described the record as 'a little feral', suggesting a bolder, rawer direction than her previous work. The announcement has sent fans into a frenzy ahead of what could be one of the biggest album drops of 2026.
articles.content:
Ariana Grande just announced a new album and the way she described it will have you counting down the days. The album is called Petal and it drops on July 31. Grande shared the news in an Instagram video and did not hold back on the details.

She admitted she had been too shy to put this record out. Then, in her words, she just thought 'F-ck it'. That kind of behind-the-scenes honesty from Grande is rare, and it tells you a lot about what to expect. This is not a carefully calculated pop rollout. This feels personal.

Grande also called Petal 'a little feral'. That phrase is doing a lot of work. Her last few albums — thank u, next, Positions and the Grammy-winning Eternal Sunshine — all had a polished, emotionally precise quality. 'A little feral' suggests Grande is letting something looser and more instinctive come through this time.

Grande has been one of the biggest pop artists on the planet for over a decade, but her profile exploded further after her acclaimed performance in the Wicked film adaptation. Whatever headspace she is in right now feels electric. If Petal is the result of her pushing past her own hesitation and going for it, that is a very exciting thing for pop music.

July 31 is not far away. Mark the date.
keyPoints: Ariana Grande's new album Petal is out on July 31, 2026
Grande revealed in an Instagram video she was initially too shy to release the record
She described Petal as 'a little feral', hinting at a rawer sound than previous albums
Grande said she pushed past her nerves by simply thinking 'F-ck it'
The announcement follows Grande's massive year off the back of the Wicked film

articles.title: Chalamet, Messi and Bad Bunny Star in Adidas 'Backyard Legends'
articles.summary: Adidas has dropped 'Backyard Legends,' a five-minute cinematic short film for the 2026 FIFA World Cup starring Timothée Chalamet alongside Lionel Messi, Bad Bunny, Jude Bellingham, Lamine Yamal, Trinity Rodman, David Beckham, Zinedine Zidane and Alessandro Del Piero. Created by agency Lola USA and directed by Mark Molloy, it is one of the most star-packed sports ads in recent memory. The Wall Street Journal has reported adidas expects the World Cup to generate around €250 million in product revenue.
articles.content:
Adidas just dropped what might be the most ambitious sports campaign in years, and it feels less like an ad and more like a short film you would actually seek out. 'Backyard Legends' runs over five minutes and it earns every second.

The setup is simple but brilliant. Timothée Chalamet plays a guy who needs to put together a crew capable of beating Clive, Ruthie and Isaak, an undefeated local trio whose backyard football legend has survived for generations. On the way there, through Chalamet's narration, you meet the full cast: Lionel Messi, Bad Bunny, Jude Bellingham, Lamine Yamal, Trinity Rodman, Zinedine Zidane, David Beckham and Alessandro Del Piero. Cameos from Ousmane Dembélé, Raphinha, Pedri, Florian Wirtz and Santiago Gimenez fill out the world even further. The whole thing ends with the two sides facing each other on a cage pitch, crowd buzzing, and three words on screen: 'You Got This.'

What makes 'Backyard Legends' land is that it does not just flex its cast. It has a story. Chalamet's character actually builds a team through the film, settling on Bellingham, Rodman and Yamal as his three, before the inevitable showdown. The 90s aesthetic throughout, complete with analogue tech, era-defining haircuts and terrace fashion, taps into the kind of nostalgia the 2026 World Cup naturally carries. The tournament returns to North American soil for the first time since the US hosted it in 1994, which is the exact era the film lovingly evokes.

Chalamet was clearly all in. He said he grew up playing at Pier 40 dreaming about Beckham's free kicks, Del Piero's goals and Zidane's volleys. Messi added that his game was born in the backyard in his hometown Rosario with no pressure, just freedom. The quotes feel real because they are rooted in actual footballer mythology rather than marketing speak.

Adidas created 'Backyard Legends' through agency Lola USA, directed by Mark Molloy through Smuggler. It is the centrepiece of a World Cup marketing push that adidas has been building since late last year, when it launched the official tournament ball at an event at Brooklyn Bridge Park. With the FIFA World Cup 2026 kicking off on June 11, the timing is right on the money.
keyPoints: Adidas released 'Backyard Legends,' a five-minute World Cup 2026 short film
Timothée Chalamet leads a cast including Messi, Bad Bunny, Beckham, Zidane and more
The film was created by Lola USA and directed by Mark Molloy through Smuggler
The 2026 FIFA World Cup kicks off on June 11 in North America
Adidas expects the World Cup to generate around €250 million in product revenue

articles.title: White House Calls Mark Hamill 'Sick' Over AI Trump Grave Image
articles.summary: Mark Hamill posted an AI-generated image on Bluesky showing Donald Trump lying in a grave with the caption "If Only," prompting the White House to call the Star Wars actor "one sick individual." Hamill later deleted the post and clarified he was actually wishing Trump the opposite of dead. The incident landed in an especially charged moment, coming fewer than two weeks after an attempted assassination of Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.
articles.content:
<cite index="15-1">Hamill posted on his Bluesky account an image of a prone Trump surrounded by daisies, lying in front of a headstone with the dates "1946-2024." A text caption on the image says "If Only."</cite> <cite index="16-5,16-6">In the post's caption, Hamill wrote: "If Only — He should live long enough to witness his inevitable devastating loss in the midterms, be held accountable for his unprecedented corruption, impeached, convicted and humiliated for his countless crimes. Long enough to realize he'll be disgraced in the history books, forevermore."</cite>

<cite index="18-8">In response, the White House's Rapid Response 47 communications account took to X, sharing a screenshot of the actor's post and calling him "one sick individual."</cite> <cite index="14-9">The White House wrote that "this kind of rhetoric is exactly what has inspired three assassination attempts in two years against our President."</cite> Mark Hamill is best known for playing Luke Skywalker in the Star Wars film franchise and has been one of the most vocal celebrity critics of Donald Trump since his first election win.

<cite index="20-5">Hamill's Bluesky post against Trump came fewer than two weeks after the White House Correspondents' Association dinner, which was interrupted by a shooter who attempted to assassinate Trump.</cite> <cite index="16-12">The suspect, Cole Tomas Allen, was charged with attempting to assassinate the president and other crimes.</cite> That timing added serious fuel to the backlash, with critics arguing that any imagery depicting Trump dead was dangerous regardless of the intent behind it.

<cite index="15-2,15-3">Hamill deleted the image from his Bluesky account and in a new post wrote: "Accurate Edit for Clarity: 'He should live long enough to… be held accountable for his… crimes.' Actually, I was wishing him the opposite of dead, but apologize if you found the image inappropriate."</cite> <cite index="19-13">Hamill, a vocal Democrat supporter, had also been invited by former President Barack Obama just this week to visit his presidential library in Chicago.</cite> The story quickly became the latest flashpoint in the ongoing culture war between Hollywood and the Trump administration.
keyPoints: Mark Hamill posted an AI image on Bluesky of Trump lying in a grave with the caption "If Only."
The White House's Rapid Response 47 account called Hamill "one sick individual" on X.
The post came fewer than two weeks after an attempted assassination of Trump at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.
Hamill deleted the image and clarified he was wishing Trump the opposite of dead.
Hamill had also visited Barack Obama's presidential library in Chicago the same week.

articles.title: Meet the Fab Four: Hamburg Days Reveals Its Beatles Cast
articles.summary: The BBC and ZDF drama series Hamburg Days has revealed who will play John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison and the rest of the early Beatles lineup as cameras start rolling across Hamburg, Munich and Liverpool. Rhys Mannion leads as John Lennon, with Ellis Murphy as Paul McCartney and Harvey Brett as George Harrison in the six-part series. It is one of the most anticipated music biopics in years, arriving just ahead of Sam Mendes' four-film Beatles cinematic event due in 2028.
articles.content:
The wait is over. Hamburg Days, the BBC and ZDF drama about The Beatles before they were The Beatles, has finally revealed the faces behind the Fab Four — and cameras are already rolling.

Rhys Mannion, known for his work in It Is in Us All, will play John Lennon. Ellis Murphy takes on Paul McCartney, Harvey Brett plays George Harrison, and Louis Landau steps into the role of Stu Sutcliffe, the band's original bassist who left before Ringo Starr joined. Patrick Gilmore plays original drummer Pete Best, Luna Jordan is Astrid Kirchherr, and Casper von Bulow portrays Klaus Voormann.

If those last two names are unfamiliar, here is the quick version. Astrid Kirchherr was a German photographer who became close with the band in Hamburg and helped shape their iconic look. Klaus Voormann was an artist and musician who later designed the cover of The Beatles' Revolver album. Hamburg Days is actually based on Voormann's autobiography, and Voormann himself, now 87, is a consultant on the show.

The series is set in the smoke-filled clubs of Hamburg's St. Pauli district in the early 1960s, where a scrappy group of Liverpool teenagers were grinding through late-night gigs before anyone outside Germany knew their names. Showrunner Christian Schwochow, who directed episodes of The Crown on Netflix, is at the helm, with BAFTA-winning composer David Holmes curating the music. Head writer Jamie Carragher, a Succession alumnus, wrote the scripts.

Hamburg Days will air on BBC One and BBC iPlayer in the UK and ZDF in Germany, with AGC handling international sales. No air date has been set yet. The series is arriving at a moment of peak Beatles fever — Sam Mendes is separately making a four-film cinematic Beatles event due in April 2028, with each film focused on a different band member. Hamburg Days gets there first, and it has the cast to make it count.
keyPoints: Rhys Mannion plays John Lennon, Ellis Murphy plays Paul McCartney, Harvey Brett plays George Harrison
Louis Landau plays Stu Sutcliffe and Patrick Gilmore plays Pete Best, the band's pre-fame lineup
The six-part series is based on Klaus Voormann's autobiography, with Voormann as a consultant
The Crown's Christian Schwochow is showrunner and Succession's Jamie Carragher is head writer
Hamburg Days will air on BBC One and BBC iPlayer with no release date yet announced

articles.title: Boards of Canada Return With 'Introit' and 'Prophecy At 1420 MHz'
articles.summary: Boards of Canada have shared two new songs, 'Introit' and 'Prophecy At 1420 MHz', ahead of their album Inferno, which drops on May 29th via Warp Records. This is the Scottish electronic duo's first album in 13 years, making it one of the most anticipated releases in the electronic music world. The two new tracks give fans their first proper taste of what the long-awaited comeback record sounds like.
articles.content:
Boards of Canada are back, and the wait is almost over. The Scottish duo, made up of brothers Marcus Eoin and Michael Sandison, have just unveiled two new songs: 'Introit' and 'Prophecy At 1420 MHz'. Both tracks are out now and available to stream, serving as the clearest preview yet of what Inferno, their first album in 13 years, is going to sound like when it arrives on May 29th through Warp Records.

If you are new to Boards of Canada, here is the quick version. They are widely considered one of the most influential electronic acts ever made. Their albums Music Has the Right to Children and Geogaddi are the kind of records that producers and musicians still study today. Their last full-length, Tomorrow's Harvest, came out in 2013 and was treated as a genuine cultural event. Then they went quiet. Very quiet.

The title Inferno and the track name 'Prophecy At 1420 MHz' (1420 MHz being the frequency of hydrogen in space, a signal used in the search for extraterrestrial intelligence) both suggest the duo is leaning into something cosmic and maybe a little ominous. That fits perfectly with the Boards of Canada sound: warm but unsettling, nostalgic but slightly wrong in a way you cannot quite place.

The 13-year gap between albums is a long time by any measure, but Boards of Canada have always operated on their own timeline. They do not tour, they rarely speak to the press, and they release music when they feel like it. That mystery is a huge part of why fans are so devoted.

May 29th cannot come fast enough. Stream 'Introit' and 'Prophecy At 1420 MHz' now and start forming your opinions before everyone else.
keyPoints: Boards of Canada have released two new songs: 'Introit' and 'Prophecy At 1420 MHz'
Their album Inferno comes out on May 29th via Warp Records
Inferno is their first album in 13 years, following Tomorrow's Harvest in 2013
The track title 'Prophecy At 1420 MHz' references the hydrogen frequency used in the search for extraterrestrial life
Both songs are available to stream now
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Boards of Canada electronic music duo

articles.title: Sam Altman's 'Still Don't Want Me?' Texts After OpenAI Ouster Revealed
articles.summary: Court documents from a 2026 trial have exposed the frantic texts Sam Altman sent after being fired as OpenAI CEO in November 2023, including a desperate plea to return. Interim CEO Mira Murati shut him down bluntly, telling him the board wanted him 'gone.' The reveal gives us the most unfiltered look yet at one of tech's most dramatic boardroom collapses.
articles.content:
In November 2023, Sam Altman was abruptly fired as CEO of OpenAI by its board. The news shocked Silicon Valley. Within days, almost the entire staff threatened to quit unless Altman was reinstated, and he was back in the job just five days later. It was wild to watch from the outside. Now, thanks to a trial in 2026, we finally know what it looked like from the inside.

Trial documents have revealed the texts Altman sent in the hours and days after he was pushed out. The messages show a man who was not ready to accept the decision. In one exchange, Altman reportedly asked interim CEO Mira Murati, 'Still don't want me?' It is a raw and surprisingly human moment from one of the most powerful people in tech.

Murati's response was not encouraging. She told Altman the board had made up its mind and that they wanted him 'gone.' That is a hard thing to read in any context, let alone when the job in question is leading the most talked-about AI company on the planet. The exchange shows just how personal and chaotic those five days really were, despite how cleanly the whole saga was eventually resolved in public.

The texts matter beyond the gossip factor. OpenAI is now at the centre of some of the biggest legal and political debates in the world, from its structure as a company to its relationship with Microsoft and its pivot toward for-profit status. Any light shed on how its leadership actually behaves behind closed doors is relevant to all of that. Sam Altman runs one of the most consequential organisations in history, and for a few days in 2023, he was reduced to sending 'still don't want me?' messages on his phone.
keyPoints: Trial documents revealed Sam Altman's private texts after his November 2023 OpenAI firing
Altman asked interim CEO Mira Murati 'Still don't want me?' in a plea to return
Murati told Altman the board had made up its mind and wanted him gone
Altman was reinstated just five days after being fired, following a near-staff walkout
The texts offer the most candid glimpse yet into one of tech's most dramatic leadership crises

articles.title: Shakira and Burna Boy Unveil 'Dai Dai' as the FIFA World Cup 2026 Anthem
articles.summary: Shakira has revealed 'Dai Dai', the official song for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, made with Nigerian superstar Burna Boy — dropping the full track on May 14. The teaser, filmed at Rio de Janeiro's iconic Maracanã Stadium, has already sent social media into a frenzy. This marks Shakira's fourth World Cup musical moment, cementing her status as the undisputed queen of tournament anthems.
articles.content:
Shakira is back for the World Cup and the internet cannot handle it. The Colombian superstar has unveiled 'Dai Dai', the official song for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, created alongside Nigerian Afrobeats giant Burna Boy. The full track drops on May 14, but the teaser alone has already broken the internet.

Shakira posted a one-minute clip on Instagram filmed at the legendary Maracanã Stadium in Rio de Janeiro. The video shows her in the yellow and blue of Brazil's national kit, surrounded by dancers in team colours and four footballs representing each World Cup she has been a part of. The title 'Dai Dai' is an Italian expression meaning 'come on, come on' — basically the perfect football chant energy.

The song blends Shakira's signature Latin-pop style with Burna Boy's Afrobeats influence, and early reactions suggest it has the infectious, stadium-ready energy that made 'Waka Waka (This Time for Africa)' such an all-time classic. That 2010 South Africa anthem now has 4.5 billion views on YouTube and is widely considered the greatest World Cup song ever made. Shakira is genuinely the only artist who could attempt to top her own legacy here.

This is actually Shakira's fourth involvement in a FIFA tournament. She performed 'Hips Don't Lie' at the 2006 World Cup final in Germany, delivered 'Waka Waka' for South Africa in 2010, and sang 'La La La' at the 2014 closing ceremony in Brazil. No other artist comes close to that record.

The timing is perfect too. Shakira recently performed for more than two million people at a free concert at Copacabana beach in Rio de Janeiro and is riding enormous global momentum from her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour. The 2026 World Cup kicks off on June 11, co-hosted by the United States, Canada and Mexico — the biggest tournament in history with 48 teams. 'Dai Dai' might just be the sound of the whole summer.
keyPoints: Shakira's official FIFA World Cup 2026 song is called 'Dai Dai', featuring Burna Boy
The full song releases on May 14, with the teaser filmed at Maracanã Stadium in Rio de Janeiro
'Dai Dai' is an Italian phrase meaning 'come on, come on'
This is Shakira's fourth musical contribution to a FIFA World Cup tournament
The 2026 World Cup runs from June 11 to July 19 across the US, Canada and Mexico

articles.title: Billie Eilish and James Cameron Make Concert Film Magic in 3D
articles.summary: Billie Eilish and James Cameron co-directed Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D), a full 3D concert film released in cinemas on May 8, 2026. It is Eilish's third concert film and the first time Cameron has co-directed a project since 2005. The film captures Eilish's sold-out run at Manchester's Co-op Live arena in July 2025 in front of crowds of over 23,000 fans a night.
articles.content:
Two Oscar winners walk into an arena. That is not the setup of a joke. <cite index="2-2">Billie Eilish's new concert movie Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D) is the rare film directed by two Oscar winners: James Cameron and Eilish herself.</cite> It is in cinemas right now, and it is a genuinely unusual collaboration.

How did this even happen? <cite index="6-13,6-14">The project was Cameron's idea. He emailed Eilish's mother, Maggie Baird, a friend of his wife via their shared interest in plant-based diets and environmentalism, and suggested they shoot Eilish's Hit Me Hard and Soft tour in 3D.</cite> <cite index="1-7">It is the first James Cameron film to have a co-director since Aliens of the Deep in 2005.</cite> <cite index="6-10">When Eilish first emerges on screen, she is suspended above a cube built of LED screens, surrounded by a sold-out crowd of over 23,000 fans in the center of the UK's largest arena, Manchester's Co-op Live.</cite>

<cite index="2-6">Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D) is her third concert film, after the animation-heavy 2021 Happier Than Ever: A Love Letter to Los Angeles and the 2023 Live at the O2.</cite> <cite index="2-12,2-13">She revisits her classic 2018 debut for a rowdy "Bad Guy" and a fantastically creepy version of "Bury A Friend," but the show focuses mainly on the confessional tunes from her later albums, Happier Than Ever and Hit Me Hard and Soft.</cite> The setlist also spans her collaboration with Charli XCX on "Guess" and her Oscar-winning "What Was I Made For?"

<cite index="2-14,2-15,2-16">The film is full of interview footage of Eilish perched on a couch backstage talking to Cameron, who is hoisting a giant camera on his shoulder, somewhat awkwardly and to comic effect. Cameron has always been a technical wizard not necessarily known for the human touch, but he labours valiantly to speak the language of pop music on her terms.</cite> <cite index="10-14">"The credits are gonna read, 'Directed by Billie Eilish', and then in small print below, 'and James Cameron,'" he jokes to her early on.</cite>

Reviews are largely positive, with most critics agreeing the 3D format genuinely adds something. <cite index="2-24">The most powerful sequence comes late in the show when Eilish, guitar in hand, performs a trio of her most touching songs, "Your Power," "Skinny," and "TV," hitting high notes of raw emotion.</cite> <cite index="6-27">At nearly two hours of runtime, it passes like a zephyr, a thrill ride so fun it feels too short.</cite> Not every critic agrees though. Some have called out the over-use of close-ups of crying audience members as a cliché that gets in the way of the performance itself.
keyPoints: Hit Me Hard and Soft: The Tour (Live in 3D) opens in cinemas May 8, 2026.
It is co-directed by both Billie Eilish and James Cameron, two Oscar winners.
Cameron reached out to Eilish's mother to propose the 3D project.
The film was shot at Eilish's sold-out run at Manchester's Co-op Live in July 2025.
It is Eilish's third concert film and Cameron's first co-directed project since 2005.
