articles.title: Valverde Hospitalized After Fight With Tchouameni Rocks Real Madrid
articles.summary: Real Madrid have opened disciplinary proceedings against Federico Valverde and Aurelien Tchouameni after a series of physical altercations between the two players, one of which was serious enough to send Valverde to hospital. The bust-ups point to a fracture in the dressing room at the Bernabeu at a critical point in the season. It is a rare and alarming sign of discord inside one of the world's biggest clubs.
articles.content:
Things have gotten ugly inside the Real Madrid dressing room. Real Madrid have opened disciplinary proceedings against Federico Valverde and Aurelien Tchouameni after multiple physical confrontations between the two players, including one that was serious enough to leave Valverde requiring hospital treatment. That is not a training ground scuffle you brush under the rug.

Valverde is a Uruguayan midfielder who has been one of Real Madrid's most important players over the last several seasons, known for his engine, his physicality, and his big-game performances. Tchouameni is a French defensive midfielder who arrived at the club in 2022 for a reported fee of around €80 million and has become a key figure in the squad. When two players that central to a club's plans end up in a fight serious enough to send one of them to hospital, it is a major story.

Real Madrid are not a club that tolerates public disorder lightly. The fact that formal disciplinary proceedings have been opened signals that the club sees this as more than a one-off flashpoint. The description of "a series of bust-ups" between the two players suggests this tension has been building for some time, not a single moment of madness in the heat of a training session.

The timing could not be worse. Real Madrid have been navigating a demanding stretch of fixtures, and internal unrest is the last thing Carlo Ancelotti's squad needs. How the club handles the proceedings against Valverde and Tchouameni, and whether either player faces a suspension or a transfer push as a result, will be one of the biggest subplots around the Bernabeu in the weeks ahead.
keyPoints: Real Madrid have opened disciplinary proceedings against both Federico Valverde and Aurelien Tchouameni
The dispute involved multiple physical altercations between the two players
One fight was serious enough to send Valverde to hospital
Tchouameni joined Real Madrid in 2022 for around €80 million
The incident points to significant dressing room unrest at a key point in the season

articles.title: Artists Strike Shuts Venice Biennale Pavilions Over Israel
articles.summary: A 24-hour pro-Palestinian strike organized by the Art Not Genocide Alliance closed multiple pavilions at the 2026 Venice Biennale on Friday, May 8, making it the first organized strike in the event's history. The action is the latest flashpoint in a weeks-long battle over Israel's inclusion in the world's most prestigious art event. The upheaval also includes a jury that resigned en masse and an awards structure that has been scrapped entirely.
articles.content:
The 2026 Venice Biennale opened its doors this week to one of the most politically charged atmospheres the 130-year-old event has ever seen. On Friday, May 8, the Art Not Genocide Alliance (ANGA) carried out a 24-hour strike across the cultural sector, shutting down pavilions and marking what organizers called the first-ever organized strike within the Biennale. The action targeted Israel's continued participation in the global art exhibition, which runs through November 2026.

The lead-up to the strike was already dramatic. Earlier in the week, hundreds of protesters gathered outside Israel's temporary pavilion in the Arsenale, the complex of former Venetian shipyards that serves as one of the Biennale's two main venues. Israel's regular pavilion in the Giardini remains closed for renovation, and the Biennale gave Israel a new spot in the Arsenale rather than requiring it to rent privately. ANGA called that move "active institutional support for a state committing genocide." Security guards closed the Israeli pavilion for more than two hours during the preview period, with Italian police standing guard at the doors.

The tension goes even deeper. ANGA's open letter demanding Israel's exclusion has gathered 236 signatures, including 18 entire national pavilion teams, 113 artists, 38 curators, and 85 art workers. Signatories include artists Brian Eno, Lubaina Himid, Alfredo Jaar, and Yto Barrada. The Biennale's five-member international jury had already announced it would not award prizes to countries "whose leaders are currently charged with crimes against humanity by the International Criminal Court" — effectively excluding both Israel and Russia. That decision prompted Israeli pavilion artist Belu-Simion Fainaru to file legal warnings alleging antisemitism and discrimination with the Biennale, the Italian Ministry of Culture, and the Italian Prime Minister's office. The jury then resigned abruptly, leading the Biennale Foundation to scrap the historic Golden Lion awards altogether and replace them with "Visitor Lions" decided by public vote.

The strike has also split participants internally. Slovenia's Nonument Group, representing their country in the Arsenale, chose to dim the lights and replace their sound installation with a livestream of Palestinian radio station Radio Alhara. Meanwhile, Ecuador's Tawana Collective weighed joining the strike against its own urgent mission to spotlight colonialism in the Amazon. As Nika Grabar of the Nonument Group put it, the strike reflects "a deep structural crisis for the Biennale." ANGA, for its part, drew a pointed historical comparison: the Venice Biennale excluded apartheid South Africa from 1968 to 1993, and they argue the same principle should apply now. The Biennale's official response was that the strike actions "do not involve the institution's staff or organisation" and that it remains committed to "freedom of expression and the plurality of opinions."
keyPoints: ANGA organized the first-ever 24-hour strike in Venice Biennale history on May 8, closing multiple pavilions in protest of Israel's inclusion
236 artists, curators, and art workers signed a letter demanding Israel's exclusion, including Brian Eno, Lubaina Himid, and Alfredo Jaar
Israel was given a temporary venue in the Arsenale after its permanent Giardini pavilion closed for renovation — ANGA called this "active institutional support"
The Biennale jury resigned after Israeli pavilion artist Belu-Simion Fainaru filed legal threats alleging antisemitism, leading the Biennale to cancel the historic Golden Lion awards
Russia's pavilion also sparked controversy, with the EU threatening to cut 2 million euros in funding over its inclusion

articles.title: Epstein's Vault Note Released: "Time to Say Goodbye"
articles.summary: A handwritten note purportedly written by Jeffrey Epstein before his first suicide attempt in July 2019 has finally been made public after years locked in a courthouse vault. U.S. District Judge Kenneth Karas unsealed the one-page document on Wednesday, May 6, following a petition from the New York Times. The cryptic note, which is unsigned and undated, reads in part "They investigated me for month – found NOTHING!!!" and ends with the words "NO FUN. NOT WORTH IT!!" underlined.
articles.content:
Jeffrey Epstein's purported suicide note is finally public. U.S. District Judge Kenneth Karas ordered the one-page handwritten document unsealed on Wednesday, May 6, after the New York Times petitioned for its release. The note had been locked away in a courthouse vault for years as sealed evidence in the criminal case of Epstein's former cellmate, Nicholas Tartaglione.

Here is what the note actually says. Written on a yellow legal pad and difficult to read in places, it is unsigned and undated. It reads, in part: "They investigated me for month – found NOTHING!!!" It goes on to say it is "a treat to be able to chose ones time to say goodbye." It closes with the words "NO FUN" and "NOT WORTH IT!!" both underlined.

Tartaglione, a former police officer who was convicted of quadruple murder, claims he found the note tucked inside a book in their shared cell after Epstein was found on the floor on July 23, 2019, with a strip of bedsheet around his neck. That incident happened about three weeks before Epstein was found dead in his cell at the Metropolitan Correctional Center in Manhattan on August 10, 2019. After the July 23 incident, Epstein told a jail psychologist he had "no interest in killing myself" and denied being suicidal, saying suicide was against his religion and calling himself a "coward" who did not like pain.

The note's authenticity has not been confirmed. The Department of Justice told reporters it was seeing the note for the first time. Judge Karas did not rule on whether the note is genuine. He simply found it was a judicial document the public has the right to access. Epstein's brother Mark Epstein has called the note illegitimate, saying the July incident was not a suicide attempt at all. Tartaglione, for his part, has said he revived Epstein with CPR that night and that handwriting experts had looked at the note. The note was submitted as part of Tartaglione's case, where it sat under seal amid a dispute over his legal representation and was never seen by federal investigators. Despite releasing millions of Epstein-related documents in recent years, the Department of Justice had no record of it until now.
keyPoints: Judge Kenneth Karas unsealed the note on May 6, 2026 after a New York Times petition
The note reads in part "They investigated me for month – found NOTHING!!!" and ends with "NO FUN. NOT WORTH IT!!"
Epstein's former cellmate Nicholas Tartaglione, a convicted murderer, claims he found it tucked in a book after Epstein's July 23, 2019 suicide attempt
The note is unsigned, undated, and has not been authenticated by the DOJ or any official body
Epstein denied being suicidal to a psychologist the day after the July incident, and died in his cell on August 10, 2019

articles.title: Bumble Kills the Swipe and Its Women-First Rule in 2026
articles.summary: Bumble CEO Whitney Wolfe Herd confirmed on May 7 that Bumble will scrap swiping entirely, calling what comes next "revolutionary for the category." The overhaul also kills Bumble's signature rule that required women to message first in heterosexual matches. The big catch: Wolfe Herd wouldn't say exactly what replaces swiping, but Bumble's AI assistant Bee is the most likely answer.
articles.content:
Bumble is about to look nothing like the app you know. <cite index="2-2">Bumble CEO Whitney Wolfe Herd confirmed in an interview with Axios on Thursday that Bumble will get rid of swiping, the defining feature of 2010s dating apps.</cite> Her exact words were a tease: <cite index="5-4">"We are going to be saying goodbye to the swipe and hello to something that I believe is revolutionary for the category."</cite> She just wouldn't say what that something actually is.

Alongside killing the swipe, Bumble is dropping another signature move. <cite index="6-4">Bumble is also dropping its women-go-first rule, which required women to message first within 24 hours in heterosexual matches.</cite> <cite index="5-7">"We will not force one gender over another to do something first," Wolfe Herd said, though she added that the app will preserve "the essence of what was always meant to be women making the first move."</cite> Both changes are expected to roll out in select markets starting in Q4 2026, so you will still be swiping for a while yet.

The clues about what comes next point squarely at AI. <cite index="6-9">Earlier this year, Bumble introduced Bee, an AI assistant designed to interview new users, recommend matches, suggest date ideas, and gather feedback to improve future recommendations.</cite> <cite index="8-17">In a feature called "Dates," Bee may even explain why two people are a good fit before they connect.</cite> Wolfe Herd has described AI as <cite index="7-26">"a supercharger to love and relationships."</cite> That said, <cite index="2-5">Gen Z is trending more negative toward in-your-face AI features, and Wolfe Herd has expressed interest in more extreme futures, like having personal AI bots that date other AI bots for you.</cite>

The timing of this overhaul is no accident. <cite index="4-4">In its Q1 2026 earnings, Bumble reported a 21.1% year-over-year decline in paying users to 3.2 million, contributing to a 14.1% drop in revenue to $212.4 million.</cite> <cite index="8-12">Bumble has framed this as a deliberate shift toward higher-quality, more intentional users</cite>, but a shrinking paying base is hard to spin forever. Bumble is betting its entire future on the idea that smarter, AI-driven matchmaking beats the endless scroll. Whether Gen Z buys it is the real question.

The rest of the dating app world is watching closely. <cite index="6-14,6-15">Tinder, the top dating app globally, has launched in-person singles events and virtual speed dating sessions to curb swipe fatigue, and even partnered with Sam Altman's biometric company World to let users verify their humanity by staring into an orb.</cite> <cite index="6-18,6-19">Hinge, which never used swipes to begin with and instead asks users to like a photo or respond to a prompt before matching, has become the financial bright spot in the dating space, which probably hasn't gone unnoticed by its competitors.</cite> Bumble is making a massive bet that the swipe era is over. Now it just has to prove it has something better.
keyPoints: Bumble CEO Whitney Wolfe Herd confirmed swiping will be gone by Q4 2026, replaced by something she calls 'revolutionary' but hasn't fully revealed.
Bumble's famous women-message-first rule in heterosexual matches is also being scrapped.
AI assistant Bee is the most likely replacement, designed to interview users, recommend matches, and explain why two people are compatible.
Bumble lost 21.1% of paying users in Q1 2026, dropping to 3.2 million, making this overhaul a business necessity.
Gen Z skepticism toward overt AI features means the redesign is a high-stakes gamble.

articles.title: Cape Fear Trailer: Bardem and Adams in Apple TV's June Thriller
articles.summary: Apple TV has dropped the full trailer for Cape Fear, a 10-episode psychological horror limited series starring Javier Bardem as convicted killer Max Cady and Amy Adams and Patrick Wilson as the attorney couple he targets after walking out of prison. The series premieres June 5 with two episodes, then drops new episodes every Friday through July 31. Martin Scorsese and Steven Spielberg, who directed and produced the iconic 1991 film respectively, are both back as executive producers on this new adaptation.
articles.content:
Cape Fear is one of Hollywood's most remade stories, and it is about to get its biggest reinvention yet. Apple TV has released the full trailer for its upcoming Cape Fear limited series, and it looks genuinely unsettling. The 10-episode psychological horror thriller premieres June 5 with its first two episodes, followed by new episodes every Friday through July 31.

For those unfamiliar with the source material: Cape Fear is based on John D. MacDonald's 1957 novel The Executioners. It was first adapted into a 1962 film starring Robert Mitchum and Gregory Peck, then famously remade in 1991 by Martin Scorsese with Robert De Niro, Nick Nolte, and Jessica Lange. The premise is simple and terrifying. A violent criminal gets out of prison and goes after the lawyers who put him there. In this Apple TV version, Javier Bardem plays Max Cady and Amy Adams and Patrick Wilson play married attorneys Anna and Tom Bowden.

The trailer opens with Bardem's Cady, fresh out of prison, locking eyes with someone and asking: "What is your worst fear?" Later he approaches Adams' Anna at a social event. She scoffs when he asks if she is afraid of him. Then we cut to Anna asking her husband: "Is there any way Max could know about what we did?" That one line tells you this version goes somewhere deeper than the original films.

Javier Bardem spoke about what makes his take on Max Cady different from both Robert Mitchum and Robert De Niro's iconic versions. He said Cady in this series "doesn't want to rush anything" and that "in both Mitchum and De Niro's amazing performances, he was more somebody that was always bringing a physical threat." In this series, Bardem says, there is "something we cannot really put our finger on until a later point in the story" that makes Cady truly unpredictable.

The creative team behind Cape Fear is formidable. Nick Antosca, who previously created Channel Zero and A Friend of the Family, wrote and showruns the series. Scorsese and Spielberg both serve as executive producers, marking the first time the two Hollywood legends have collaborated on a television project. Adams and Bardem also executive produce alongside the directing team. Pilot director Morten Tyldum, known for The Imitation Game, also serves as executive producer. Cape Fear arrives June 5 on Apple TV.
keyPoints: Cape Fear is a 10-episode Apple TV psychological horror limited series premiering June 5
Javier Bardem plays Max Cady, Amy Adams and Patrick Wilson play the attorney couple he terrorises
Martin Scorsese and Steven Spielberg both executive produce, their first ever TV collaboration
Nick Antosca, creator of Channel Zero and A Friend of the Family, wrote and showruns the series
New episodes drop every Friday on Apple TV through July 31
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Cape Fear 2026 TV series Apple TV

articles.title: Charli XCX Says the Dance Floor Is Dead on New Single 'Rock Music'
articles.summary: Charli XCX is back with a sharp left turn. Her new single 'Rock Music' opens with the line 'I think the dance floor is dead, so now we're making rock music,' signalling a bold genre pivot from the pop star behind last year's culture-swallowing Brat era. It is her most provocative statement yet about where pop is heading — and where she wants to take it.
articles.content:
Charli XCX does not do quiet. Ever since her 2024 album Brat turned an entire summer neon green and made 'brat girl' a genuine cultural shorthand, everyone has been watching to see what she does next. Now we know. The new single is called 'Rock Music,' and it comes with a mission statement baked right into the lyrics.

The opening line says it all: 'I think the dance floor is dead, so now we're making rock music.' That is not just a lyric. That is a provocation. Charli XCX is one of the sharpest trend-readers in pop, so when she declares something dead, people tend to listen.

The timing is interesting. Dance-pop and hyperpop have dominated the last few years, and Charli XCX was one of their biggest champions. Pivoting to rock feels like a genuine statement rather than a costume. It fits a broader mood in music right now, where artists from Sabrina Carpenter to Olivia Rodrigo have been leaning into guitar sounds to stand out from a streaming landscape flooded with electronic beats.

If Brat was about sweaty clubs and messy feelings, 'Rock Music' suggests Charli XCX is already thinking about what comes after the club. Whether this is a one-off single or a hint at a whole new sonic chapter, the internet is already spiralling. That is exactly where she wants us.
keyPoints: Charli XCX released a new single called 'Rock Music' on 8 May 2026
The opening lyric declares 'the dance floor is dead' and announces a rock pivot
The release marks a sharp genre shift from her Brat era dance-pop sound
Rock sounds are a growing trend across pop music right now
No album announcement has been made yet but the single is generating major buzz

articles.title: Skyroot Aerospace Hits $1.1B Valuation Before Historic Orbital Launch
articles.summary: Skyroot Aerospace has become India's first space tech unicorn after raising $60 million in a Series C round, pushing its valuation to $1.1 billion. The Hyderabad-based startup is now weeks away from launching Vikram-1, India's first privately developed orbital rocket. The valuation has more than doubled from $519 million in 2023, with backers including GIC, BlackRock, and Sherpalo Ventures.
articles.content:
Something big just happened in India's space race. <cite index="6-1">Skyroot Aerospace raised $60 million in a Series C funding round on May 7, 2026, vaulting the small-satellite launch vehicle developer to a valuation of $1.1 billion and making it India's first space tech unicorn.</cite> A unicorn, for context, is any privately held startup that hits a $1 billion valuation. It's a rare club, and Skyroot just walked in.

<cite index="1-3">Skyroot was founded in 2018 by former Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO) engineers Pawan Kumar Chandana and Naga Bharath Daka.</cite> <cite index="6-8">Skyroot became the first Indian private firm to launch a sub-orbital rocket with Vikram-S in November 2022, and is now preparing to fly Vikram-1, India's first private orbital rocket.</cite> The leap from sub-orbital to orbital is massive. It's the difference between touching space and actually getting to work there.

<cite index="4-5">This latest round pushes Skyroot's total funding to around $160 million, marking a sharp jump in valuation from about $519 million in 2023 to unicorn status in 2026.</cite> <cite index="2-3,2-4">The round was co-led by Sherpalo Ventures and GIC, a global institutional investor. Ram Shriram, the founder of Sherpalo and a board member of Alphabet, will be joining Skyroot's board.</cite> Having an early Google investor on your board is not a small deal.

<cite index="3-12,3-13">Skyroot will use the capital towards scaling Vikram-1 launch cadence, expanding manufacturing capacity, and accelerating the development of Vikram-2. CEO Pawan Kumar Chandana says Skyroot is aiming for four to six launches this financial year.</cite> <cite index="5-4,5-5">Around 90 percent of Skyroot's customers are not from India. Vikram-1 is 95 percent indigenous and is expected to see strong demand from the telecommunications sector.</cite>

<cite index="1-9">India's space economy is estimated at $8.4 billion and projected to grow to $44 billion by 2033, while the country had nearly 400 space tech startups as of early 2026.</cite> Skyroot is at the tip of that spear. As Chandana put it, <cite index="9-6">"We at Skyroot are excited about the upcoming Vikram-1 launch, India's first private orbital rocket, marking a significant milestone both for India and the global space sector."</cite> The countdown is on.
keyPoints: Skyroot Aerospace is now India's first space tech unicorn at a $1.1 billion valuation
The $60 million Series C was co-led by GIC and Sherpalo Ventures, with BlackRock also participating
Vikram-1, India's first private orbital rocket, is set to launch within weeks
Skyroot's valuation more than doubled from $519 million in 2023
Total funding raised now stands at $160 million across nine rounds since 2018

articles.title: Dua Lipa Crashes Tame Impala's O2 Show for Two Songs
articles.summary: Dua Lipa surprised the crowd at Tame Impala's sold-out O2 Arena show in London on 7 May, joining Kevin Parker onstage to perform two songs together. The pair have a deep creative bond — Parker co-produced Lipa's Radical Optimism album and the two have now shared a stage twice. Fans were treated to the Deadbeat track 'Afterthought' and Lipa's smash hit 'Houdini', making it one of the standout live moments of 2026 so far.
articles.content:
If you were at The O2 Arena in London on Thursday night, you witnessed something special. Tame Impala — the one-man psychedelic project of Australian Kevin Parker — was deep into his Deadbeat Tour set when Dua Lipa walked out. The crowd absolutely lost it.

The two performed 'Afterthought', a track from Tame Impala's latest album Deadbeat, before Lipa launched into her own song 'Houdini'. That track was co-written and co-produced by Parker and appears on Lipa's most recent album Radical Optimism. It felt like a celebration of everything the two of them built together in the studio.

This is not the first time these two have shared a stage. Back at Glastonbury, Parker joined Lipa during her headline set to play the iconic Tame Impala track 'The Less I Know The Better'. That moment went viral. Thursday's surprise flipped the script — this time it was Lipa stepping into Parker's world.

Parker released Deadbeat last year to huge acclaim, with the album topping the charts in Australia and the single 'End of Summer' winning Parker a Grammy Award for Best Dance/Electronic Recording in February 2026. The Deadbeat Tour is now rolling through the UK before heading to North America in July. If London was any indication, the rest of the tour is going to be something else entirely.
keyPoints: Dua Lipa made a surprise appearance at Tame Impala's O2 Arena show in London on 7 May 2026
The pair performed 'Afterthought' from Deadbeat and Lipa's own hit 'Houdini'
'Houdini' was co-written and co-produced by Kevin Parker for Lipa's Radical Optimism album
The duo previously shared a stage at Glastonbury, where Parker joined Lipa to play 'The Less I Know The Better'
Tame Impala's Deadbeat Tour continues across the UK before moving to North America in July

articles.title: Google Fitbit Air Is a $99 Screenless Tracker Taking On Whoop
articles.summary: Google unveiled the Fitbit Air on May 7, 2026, a screenless wrist tracker priced at $99.99 that goes directly after Whoop's dominance in the no-display fitness wearable space. It packs serious health sensors into a tiny pebble design and pairs with a Gemini-powered AI coach in the new Google Health app. Pre-orders are open now and the device ships May 26.
articles.content:
Google just made the case that your next fitness tracker should have no screen at all. <cite index="1-1">Google announced the Fitbit Air as a screenless fitness tracker with a low-profile and affordable $99.99 price.</cite> That puts it squarely in the same lane as Whoop, the subscription-based recovery wearable that turned a generation of gym-goers onto the idea that you do not need a display on your wrist to get serious health data.

Do not let the minimalist look fool you. <cite index="4-9">Fitbit Air is Google's smallest tracker yet, packing advanced health and fitness tracking like 24/7 heart rate, heart rhythm monitoring with Afib alerts, SpO2, resting heart rate, heart rate variability, and sleep stages and duration into a tiny, discreet pebble.</cite> <cite index="2-18,2-19">The Fitbit Air has up to a week of battery life, fast charging can deliver a full day of power in just five minutes, and it is water-resistant up to 50 meters.</cite> The whole thing weighs just 12 grams with the band attached, so you will barely notice it at night.

The software side of this launch might actually be the bigger deal. <cite index="3-9,3-10">Google is rebranding the Fitbit app and Fitbit Premium into something called Google Health, continuing the gradual shift away from the Fitbit identity. That includes a redesigned app experience built around Google Health Coach, an AI assistant powered by Gemini.</cite> <cite index="5-12">A new Sleep Score feature is included, which Google states is 15% more accurate than previous Fitbit sleep tracking thanks to a new machine learning model.</cite> After your three-month free trial that comes with every purchase, Google Health Premium runs $9.99 a month or $99.99 a year.

<cite index="4-21,4-22,4-23">Co-designed with Stephen Curry, the Fitbit Air Special Edition Performance Loop band comes in elegant rye brown with a pop of game-day orange. It features a unique water-resistant coating and a raised interior print inspired by athletic racing stripes specifically engineered to increase airflow during high-intensity movement.</cite> That edition is priced at $129.99. <cite index="1-10">The Fitbit Air is available for $99.99 starting on May 26, with pre-orders opening now.</cite>
keyPoints: Fitbit Air launches at $99.99 with no screen, shipping May 26
Tracks heart rate, SpO2, sleep, Afib alerts and more in a 12-gram pebble
Pairs with the new Gemini-powered Google Health Coach app
Sleep Score is 15% more accurate than previous Fitbit tracking
A Stephen Curry Special Edition is available for $129.99

articles.title: Mounjaro Beats Keytruda as World's Top-Selling Drug
articles.summary: Eli Lilly's diabetes and weight-loss drug Mounjaro has overtaken Merck's cancer therapy Keytruda to become the best-selling medication on the planet. Mounjaro pulled in $8.7 billion in Q1 2026 alone, compared to Keytruda's $7.9 billion, ending Keytruda's three-year run at the top. The milestone signals a seismic cultural and economic shift: a weight-loss drug has now dethroned a cancer treatment as the world's most valuable medicine.
articles.content:
A weight-loss drug just became the most powerful drug on Earth. Eli Lilly's Mounjaro, a GLP-1 medication originally approved for Type 2 diabetes, generated $8.7 billion in sales in the first quarter of 2026 alone, surpassing Merck's cancer therapy Keytruda, which brought in $7.9 billion in the same period. It is a stunning reversal that says as much about culture and body image as it does about medicine.

Keytruda had held the top spot since Q1 2023, when it knocked off AbbVie's autoimmune drug Humira. Keytruda is an immunotherapy used to treat many types of cancer. For it to be beaten by a drug that millions of people take voluntarily to lose weight is genuinely unprecedented in the history of pharmaceuticals.

Eli Lilly's numbers get even bigger when you zoom out. Mounjaro and Lilly's companion weight-loss drug Zepbound share the same active ingredient, tirzepatide. Together, those two drugs generated $36.5 billion in combined sales in 2025, far ahead of Keytruda's $31.6 billion for the full year. Mounjaro's Q1 2026 U.S. revenue alone was $4.2 billion, up 59% from a year ago, while international revenue hit $4.4 billion, driven in large part by Mounjaro being added to China's National Reimbursement Drug List, opening it up to the Chinese market. Mounjaro is now available in more than 55 countries.

For context on Keytruda's future: Merck is staring down a patent cliff, with Keytruda's patent set to expire in 2028. That pressure has pushed Merck to diversify its pipeline, though obesity is not currently part of that plan. Eli Lilly, meanwhile, recently launched another obesity treatment called Foundayo and raised its 2026 revenue outlook to between $82 billion and $85 billion, with analysts projecting the company could approach $100 billion in revenue by 2027.

This story matters beyond Wall Street. GLP-1 drugs like Mounjaro have reshaped conversations around body image, fashion sizing, beauty standards, and food culture. The fact that a drug rooted in those conversations now outsells every cancer medication on the planet is a marker of where society's attention and money are flowing. Whether you see that as progress or a red flag about healthcare priorities, it is one of the defining cultural facts of 2026.
keyPoints: Eli Lilly's Mounjaro generated $8.7 billion in Q1 2026, beating Merck's Keytruda at $7.9 billion
Keytruda had been the world's top-selling drug since Q1 2023
Mounjaro and Zepbound together made $36.5 billion in 2025, using the same active ingredient tirzepatide
Mounjaro's international growth was fueled by being added to China's National Reimbursement Drug List
Eli Lilly raised its 2026 revenue outlook to $82–$85 billion, with Foundayo also launching in the U.S.

articles.title: Hayley Williams Brings 'The Hayley Williams Show' to North and Latin America
articles.summary: Hayley Williams has announced a massive new headlining run called The Hayley Williams Show, kicking off September 3 in West Palm Beach and stretching all the way to San Juan, Puerto Rico on November 27. The tour scales up significantly from her current intimate run, with already sold-out nights at New York's Forest Hills Stadium and Los Angeles' Hollywood Bowl. Magdalena Bay and Rico Nasty will support on the North American leg, while Annie DiRusso joins for Latin America and Puerto Rico.
articles.content:
Hayley Williams is not slowing down. While she is still in the middle of her Ego Death at a Bachelorette Party tour, the Paramore frontwoman just dropped full routing details for a brand new headlining run. It is called The Hayley Williams Show, and it is a serious step up in size.

<cite index="2-4,2-5">In September, Williams will kick off The Hayley Williams Show with performances across North and Latin America, beginning on September 3 in West Palm Beach, Florida and running through November 27, when she wraps with a show in San Juan, Puerto Rico.</cite> That is a lot of ground to cover, and a lot of fans who finally get their shot at a ticket.

<cite index="2-6,2-7">The run includes two nights at New York's Forest Hills Stadium and two at Los Angeles' Hollywood Bowl, and all four of those shows were announced prior to the full tour reveal and have already sold out.</cite> If you missed those, there is still plenty of tour left. <cite index="2-8">Fans can also catch Williams in Boston, Detroit, Cincinnati, San Diego, New Orleans, Rio de Janeiro, Buenos Aires, Mexico City, and more cities worldwide.</cite>

<cite index="5-4">Magdalena Bay and Rico Nasty will support on the US leg, while Annie DiRusso will join for Latin America and Puerto Rico.</cite> As for what to expect on stage, <cite index="5-14">The Hayley Williams Show will feature material from across Williams' entire solo catalog as well as "some exciting surprises."</cite> <cite index="10-7,10-8">One dollar from each ticket sold in North America will go toward eco initiative REVERB and Support+Feed, and the new dates will include a verified fan presale, which Williams introduced on the first leg to avoid bots and scalpers.</cite>

<cite index="2-2">The Ego Death at a Bachelorette Party tour, Hayley Williams' debut solo headlining run, returned her to the small and intimate venues she grew familiar with during the early days of Paramore.</cite> <cite index="2-3">Williams has not even wrapped that tour yet, but she is already itching to get back onstage, and on even bigger ones at that.</cite> The jump from sold-out club shows to amphitheaters and iconic outdoor venues like the Hollywood Bowl tells you everything about how this solo chapter is going.
keyPoints: The Hayley Williams Show tour runs September 3 to November 27, 2026
Tour spans North America and Latin America, ending in San Juan, Puerto Rico
Two nights each at Forest Hills Stadium and the Hollywood Bowl are already sold out
Magdalena Bay and Rico Nasty support the US leg; Annie DiRusso joins for Latin America
$1 from every North American ticket goes to REVERB and Support+Feed

articles.title: Schmigadoon! Extends Broadway Run to January 2027
articles.summary: Schmigadoon!, the Broadway musical comedy that scored 12 Tony Award nominations on May 5, has announced a four-month extension that keeps it running at the Nederlander Theatre through January 3, 2027. The show, which only opened on April 20, was originally set to close on September 6, 2026, making this a 17-week addition. Schmigadoon! is now one of the hottest tickets on Broadway, tied with The Lost Boys as the season's most-nominated production.
articles.content:
Schmigadoon! just had the best week any new Broadway show could ask for. The musical comedy opened on April 20 at the Nederlander Theatre, racked up 12 Tony Award nominations on May 5, and then on May 7 announced it is extending its run all the way to January 3, 2027. Producers cited "popular demand" as the reason. What was supposed to be a September 6, 2026 closing date is now a full 17 weeks longer.

If you are not already familiar, Schmigadoon! started as an Apple TV+ series. The show is a loving parody of Golden Age Broadway musicals, the kind of big, splashy mid-20th-century productions that gave the world Oklahoma! and Guys and Dolls. The story follows a modern-day New York couple who find themselves trapped in the magical town of Schmigadoon, where every day is a musical, and only true love can get them home. The book and score are by Cinco Paul, and the show is directed and choreographed by Christopher Gattelli. Saturday Night Live's Lorne Michaels produces.

The cast is a major draw. Alex Brightman leads the company, joined by Sara Chase and Ana Gasteyer, both of whom earned individual Tony nominations for their work in the show. Schmigadoon! is nominated for Best Musical at the 79th Annual Tony Awards, where it tied with The Lost Boys at 12 nominations each to lead the entire season. The Tony Awards ceremony is hosted by Pink and airs June 7, 2026 on CBS and Paramount+ from Radio City Music Hall.

The extension is smart business. Schmigadoon! will now play through the Thanksgiving and Christmas holiday season, two of Broadway's busiest stretches of the year. For fans of the Apple TV+ series or anyone who loves classic musical theater done with a wink, this is the window to grab tickets before the Tony buzz sends prices even higher.
keyPoints: Schmigadoon! extended its Broadway run to January 3, 2027, a 17-week addition beyond its original September 6, 2026 closing date
The show earned 12 Tony Award nominations on May 5, tying with The Lost Boys as the most-nominated production of the 2025-26 season
Schmigadoon! is nominated for Best Musical at the 79th Annual Tony Awards, airing June 7 on CBS and Paramount+
The show stars Alex Brightman, Sara Chase, and Ana Gasteyer, with Sara Chase and Ana Gasteyer both receiving individual Tony nominations
Schmigadoon! is based on the Apple TV+ series, produced by Lorne Michaels with a book and score by Cinco Paul

articles.title: Kendrick Lamar and Dr. Dre Break Ground at Compton's Centennial High
articles.summary: Kendrick Lamar and Dr. Dre returned to their alma mater, Centennial High School in Compton, California, on May 7 for the groundbreaking ceremony of its new campus. They were joined by will.i.am and U.S. Rep. Maxine Waters to mark the start of construction on a building that will house more than 1,000 students. Dr. Dre addressed the crowd, calling it "a full circle moment" and joking he attended Centennial "sometimes."
articles.content:
Kendrick Lamar and Dr. Dre showed up for Compton in a big way on Thursday, May 7. The two hip-hop legends returned to Centennial High School, the school they both attended, for the groundbreaking ceremony of its brand new campus.

<cite index="2-1">The event marked the start of construction on a new school building that will house more than 1,000 students.</cite> <cite index="2-4">Also joining on the day were will.i.am, a Los Angeles native, and U.S. Rep. Maxine Waters.</cite> It was a rare public gathering of multiple generations of Southern California music royalty, all brought together by one shared ZIP code.

<cite index="1-15,1-16,1-17,1-18">During the event, Micah Ali, board president of Compton Unified School District, invited Dr. Dre to give a speech. The music mogul reflected on the significance of the moment. "Today is really nostalgic for me," said Dre. "Sometimes you hear that term 'full circle.' This is really a full circle moment for me, because I did actually attend this high school."</cite> <cite index="2-5,2-6">He joked that he was enrolled and attended "sometimes," having gone to Centennial for his freshman year in 1979 before transferring and eventually dropping out.</cite>

<cite index="2-8">"Today isn't just about a new building, it's about a new promise kept to the city that made me, point blank period," Dre told the crowd.</cite> Both artists have long put their money and presence behind Compton. <cite index="1-7">Back in 2015, Dre announced he was donating all of his artist royalties from his third studio album, Compton, to help fund a new performing arts and entertainment facility in his hometown.</cite> <cite index="1-4,1-5,1-6">Kendrick Lamar received the Key to the City in 2016. Born in Compton, he attended Centennial High as a straight-A student and also served as the 63rd Grand Marshal of the Compton Christmas Parade, with the California State Senate giving him a Generational Icon Award.</cite>

<cite index="10-7">will.i.am also participated in the ceremony, continuing his longstanding advocacy for technology access and education programs in underserved communities.</cite> Seeing three artists of this stature unite for something this grounded is a reminder that the most powerful thing fame can buy is the ability to invest back into the place that made you.
keyPoints: Kendrick Lamar and Dr. Dre attended the Centennial High School groundbreaking ceremony in Compton on May 7, 2026
The new campus will house more than 1,000 students
will.i.am and U.S. Rep. Maxine Waters also attended
Dr. Dre called it a full circle moment and joked he attended the school only sometimes
Both artists have long histories of investing in the Compton community

articles.title: Brendan Fraser Is Getting Back in Shape for The Mummy 4
articles.summary: Brendan Fraser is officially back as Rick O'Connell, confirming he's working on his fitness for The Mummy 4 twenty years after the last film. The Oscar-winning actor shared an honest, self-deprecating update with fans, saying 'doing my best' and asking them to 'wish me luck.' It's the kind of real-talk that makes you root for him even harder.
articles.content:
Twenty years is a long time. The last time Brendan Fraser suited up as Rick O'Connell, George W. Bush was in his first term and flip phones were still cool. Now, with The Mummy 4 officially in motion, Fraser is dusting off the adventure-hero persona he made famous in 1999 and telling fans exactly where he stands on the physical preparation front.

In typical Fraser fashion, the update was refreshingly honest. Asked about getting into shape for the role, he said he's "doing my best" and signed off with a "wish me luck" that felt less like false modesty and more like the kind of thing a real person says when they're genuinely putting in the work. It is the exact energy fans have come to love about him.

For those who need the backstory: Brendan Fraser played Rick O'Connell in three Mummy films between 1999 and 2008, turning the franchise into one of the most beloved adventure series of that era. After years away from the spotlight, Fraser made one of Hollywood's most celebrated comebacks when he won the Academy Award for Best Actor for The Whale in 2023. The Mummy 4 is his biggest action return yet.

The fact that Fraser is being so open about the physical challenge is part of what makes this story worth following. He is not pretending to be 30. He is a 57-year-old Oscar winner who is genuinely grinding to bring back a character people adore. That combination of vulnerability and determination? It is exactly what the internet runs on right now.
keyPoints: Brendan Fraser is reprising his role as Rick O'Connell in The Mummy 4
Fraser joked he is 'doing my best' to get in shape and asked fans to 'wish me luck'
The last Mummy film came out in 2008, making this a 20-plus year gap
Fraser won the Best Actor Oscar for The Whale in 2023 before this comeback
The role marks his biggest action return since his Hollywood resurgence

articles.title: AI Picks Spain, But 40% of Fans Are Riding With France
articles.summary: Microsoft's Copilot says Spain's La Roja will win the 2026 FIFA World Cup — but a new Bank of America survey found that 40% of soccer fans are backing France's Les Bleus instead. The tournament kicks off June 11 and runs through July 19 across the United States, Canada, and Mexico. With over 75% of the globe expected to engage with the tournament in some form, the stakes — and the debate — have never been bigger.
articles.content:
The 2026 FIFA World Cup is just weeks away, and everyone has an opinion on who will lift the trophy. But now there is a new voice in the conversation: artificial intelligence. And it disagrees with nearly half of all soccer fans on the planet.

A new Bank of America Global Research study called "The Beautiful Game: BofA's World Cup 2026 Guide" was shared with Fast Company, and it revealed a striking split. <cite index="4-8">Approximately 40% of the FIFA fans surveyed are betting on France's Les Bleus — named after the team's traditional blue jerseys — to win the whole thing.</cite> But AI has a different answer entirely. <cite index="4-9">Microsoft's Copilot thinks Spain's La Roja, or "The Red One," will take home the gold.</cite>

Spain is not exactly a surprise pick. <cite index="9-20,9-21">Spain's squad features young talents like Lamine Yamal, Pedri, and Nico Williams, alongside experienced players like Rodri and Dani Carvajal. Their possession-style football and their recent success as 2024 Euro Cup winners give them the edge in the data.</cite> France, meanwhile, carries serious weight of their own. <cite index="1-10">Didier Deschamps' side won the 2018 World Cup and lost the 2022 final on penalties, making them one of the most consistently dangerous national teams in football.</cite> That pedigree is clearly what has fans so confident in Les Bleus.

Beyond the winner prediction, the Bank of America report paints a massive picture of what this tournament means economically. <cite index="4-13">The 2026 World Cup is expected to provide a boost of up to $41 billion to the global GDP, while supporting over 800,000 jobs, including some 185,000 in the United States alone.</cite> <cite index="4-14,4-15">The tournament kicks off June 11 and runs through July 19, featuring 48 teams playing over 100 matches across three host countries in North America for the first time ever.</cite> Whether you trust the fans or the algorithm, one thing is certain: the world is watching.
keyPoints: Microsoft's Copilot AI picks Spain's La Roja to win the 2026 World Cup
A Bank of America survey found 40% of fans think France's Les Bleus will win instead
The tournament runs June 11 to July 19 across the US, Canada, and Mexico
Spain's squad includes Lamine Yamal, Pedri, and Nico Williams, backed by 2024 Euro glory
The 2026 World Cup is projected to boost global GDP by up to $41 billion

articles.title: Pokémon and Renova Launch the World's First Collectible Toilet Paper
articles.summary: Pokémon has teamed up with tissue brand Renova to release what they are calling the world's first collectible toilet paper, available now in South Korea. The rolls feature Pokémon characters including Growlithe, turning the most mundane bathroom essential into a piece of fan merchandise. It is exactly the kind of unexpected brand crossover that Pokémon has become a master of.
articles.content:
Pokémon has officially run out of things it cannot slap its branding on. The beloved franchise has partnered with tissue brand Renova to release what is being billed as the world's first collectible toilet paper, and it is available right now in South Korea.

The collaboration puts Pokémon artwork, including Growlithe, on toilet roll packaging, turning something you would normally never think twice about into a collector's item. Renova is a tissue company with a history of producing brightly coloured and novelty paper products, so in hindsight this pairing makes a strange kind of sense. Pokémon fans in South Korea can now genuinely debate whether to use the stuff or keep it sealed.

This is the kind of brand move that sounds absurd until you remember how enormous the Pokémon franchise actually is. Pokémon trading cards, clothing, food collabs, theme park attractions, and now bathroom supplies. The franchise has spent decades proving that its appeal stretches way beyond video games and anime, and every weird collab just cements that further. When Pokémon puts its name on something, people pay attention and, more importantly, people buy it.

South Korea makes a lot of sense as the launch market. The country has one of the most passionate Pokémon fan communities in Asia, and collectible culture there is enormous. Limited edition packaging and branded everyday goods regularly sell out fast. Expect this one to disappear from shelves quickly and reappear on resale sites at a significant markup. Whether or not this ever rolls out beyond South Korea, the Pokémon and Renova collaboration has already done its job: everyone is talking about it.
keyPoints: Pokémon and Renova have launched what they call the world's first collectible toilet paper
The product is available in South Korea
Pokémon characters including Growlithe appear on the rolls
The collab is the latest in a long line of unexpected Pokémon brand partnerships
Collectible culture in South Korea makes it an ideal launch market

articles.title: Angry Birds, FIFA, Dragon Quest and Silent Hill Join Gaming Hall of Fame
articles.summary: The World Video Game Hall of Fame announced its 2026 inductees: Angry Birds, FIFA, Dragon Quest, and Silent Hill. These four titles join a prestigious list of games recognized for their cultural and industry impact. Meanwhile, fan favourites Skyrim and RuneScape missed out this year.
articles.content:
The World Video Game Hall of Fame, based at The Strong National Museum of Play in Rochester, New York, picks games each year that have had a lasting influence on the industry and on popular culture. To get in, a game has to be iconic, widely played, and genuinely significant. The 2026 class is a strong one.

Angry Birds is a no-brainer. The mobile game from Rovio launched in 2009 and turned millions of people into casual gamers almost overnight. It proved that a simple physics puzzle could become a global phenomenon and helped define what mobile gaming even meant. FIFA, the long-running football simulation series from EA Sports, gets its due recognition as well. Few sports games have shaped how an entire generation watches and plays football, and FIFA's influence stretches from living rooms to esports arenas worldwide.

Dragon Quest, the foundational Japanese role-playing game series from Square Enix, is a giant in Asia and a cornerstone of the RPG genre globally. Its 1986 debut essentially wrote the rulebook that other RPGs followed for decades. Silent Hill, Konami's psychological horror series, earns its place as one of the most influential and genuinely terrifying franchises in gaming history. It helped define the survival horror genre alongside Resident Evil and left a lasting mark on storytelling in games.

Not everyone made the cut, though. Skyrim, Bethesda's beloved open-world RPG, and RuneScape, the iconic browser-based MMO, were both nominated but did not receive enough votes to join the 2026 class. Fans of both games will likely push hard for next year.
keyPoints: Angry Birds, FIFA, Dragon Quest, and Silent Hill are the 2026 World Video Game Hall of Fame inductees.
The Hall of Fame is housed at The Strong National Museum of Play in Rochester, New York.
Angry Birds helped define mobile gaming after its 2009 launch.
Dragon Quest, dating back to 1986, helped establish the RPG genre globally.
Skyrim and RuneScape were nominated but did not make the 2026 class.
